public_targets.reported_result: 95.60% overall with Chronos High; 92.60% overall with Chronos Low
public_targets.win_condition: Beat the best publicly sourced overall score with a reproducible run.
public_targets.notes: Chronos, submitted on March 17, 2026, is the highest public LongMemEvalS claim in the current source sweep. Mastra OM remains the strongest implementation-backed public claim at 94.87% with gpt-5-mini and 84.23% with gpt-4o.
public_targets.next_action: Treat Chronos as the frontier score target and Mastra OM as the strongest implementation-backed comparison point during reproduction.

public_targets.reported_result: #1 claim, exact public score not pinned in reviewed source
public_targets.win_condition: Pin exact sourced threshold, then beat it on the same task definition.
public_targets.notes: Public README claims #1, but exact numeric score was not visible in the source sweep.
public_targets.next_action: Capture exact leaderboard number from official benchmark or reproducible MemoryBench run.

public_targets.dataset_scope: published benchmark configurations from 4k to 500k context spans
public_targets.reported_result: Living benchmark with published configs and reported GoodAI agent baselines; exact single frontier number depends on configuration.
public_targets.win_condition: Beat strong baselines inside the published harness on the chosen configuration set and preserve reproducibility.
public_targets.notes: Best used as an internal core stress benchmark for long-span conversational memory and continual-memory upkeep rather than as a single public leaderboard scalar.
public_targets.next_action: Choose the first canonical configuration set, reproduce the published baseline path, and lock the scorecard contract.

public_targets.dataset_scope: 100 coherent conversations and 2,000 validated questions up to 10M tokens
public_targets.reported_result: Paper and official repo report LIGHT improving 3.5% to 12.69% over the strongest baselines depending on the backbone LLM.
public_targets.win_condition: Pin one exact public repo commit, exact dataset splits, and exact evaluation flow, then beat the strongest reported baseline on the same BEAM surface.
public_targets.notes: High-value frontier benchmark for higher-context and million-token-scale memory pressure. Public repo and datasets now exist, so the remaining job is exact reproduction pinning rather than waiting for a release.
public_targets.next_action: Pin the official repo commit, dataset sources, and evaluation flow; keep the local pilot lane as an internal regression slice rather than the main BEAM proof.

experimental_frontier_claims.reported_result: ~98.60% with an 8-variant ensemble; ~97.20% with a 12-variant decision forest; framed as ~99% frontier memory
experimental_frontier_claims.notes: User-provided Supermemory ASMR writeup describes a forthcoming experimental release rather than the main production engine. The architecture uses multi-agent ingestion, agentic search, and answer ensembles. Treat it as a high-signal frontier claim to learn from, not yet as a pinned reproducible benchmark bar.
experimental_frontier_claims.next_action: Track the promised public release, pin the exact implementation surface, then reproduce against the same LongMemEval_s path.

benchmark_inventory.scope: 500 questions; six categories plus abstention; LongMemEval_s roughly 115k tokens and about 40 sessions
10 very long conversations with QA and event summarization annotations
Living benchmark for long-term memory and continual-learning capabilities over very long conversations, with published configurations reaching up to 500k-token spans
100 coherent and topically diverse conversations with 2,000 validated questions, designed to scale up to roughly 10M tokens
benchmark_inventory.license: MIT code plus CC BY-SA 4.0 datasets

shadow_benchmarks.scope: 75,336 QA pairs across user facts, assistant facts, preferences, changing facts, implicit connections, and abstention
shadow_benchmarks.why: Guardrail against overbuilding retrieval when full context still works and against regressions on preferences, changing facts, and abstention.

open_source_memory_systems.name: Mastra Observational Memory
open_source_memory_systems.license: Apache-2.0 core plus enterprise-licensed ee paths

verdict: LongMemEval has a pinned public bar. Supermemory ASMR is tracked separately as a pending experimental frontier claim rather than a pinned reproducible target. LoCoMo remains a frontier target with an exact public threshold still to pin. GoodAI LTM Benchmark is adopted as the third official benchmark harness. BEAM is adopted as the core frontier stress benchmark, with public repo and dataset surfaces now available but exact reproduction still to pin. ConvoMem is retained as a shadow regression benchmark.
